core_mechanism: clique percolation — extracting overlapping clusters on a graph as connected unions of fully-connected subgraphs (k-cliques adjacent when they share all but one node), then matching these percolation clusters across successive network snapshots to follow their birth, growth, merging, and death over time
brief_reason: The abstract states the method is 'a new algorithm based on clique percolation' that for the first time investigates the time dependence of overlapping communities, i.e. it identifies overlapping clusters via adjacent-clique percolation and tracks them across temporal snapshots.
